reviews.title: One of the worst hotels you will ever find
reviews.text:
There is a LOT to unpack in this review so I’m going to keep it simple and put in bullet points 
- expect a minimum 30 mins wait for a buggy to take you from reception to your room after checking in. They only ever have a single buggy operating and the hotel is spread all over the Al Seef area so it’s not possible to make it to your room on foot with luggage 
- hot water runs out during peak times - you will have literally no hot water coming from the tap. Had to shower with freezing cold water at 6.30 am - no choice to complain/change rooms as we were checking out to catch a flight 
- the drainage system does not work. If you make the mistake of taking a ten minute shower, you will be standing in ankle deep water 
- completely clueless, rude and incompetent front desk staff. You make a complaint and the lady will just stare at you blankly like she cannot process what is being said. Amazed that they made it through hospitality training with that attitude 
- at check out, called for a buggy 45 mins in advance and sent my luggage out to be loaded in to my rental car. Buggy showed up quickly and we walked down with a back pack expecting to get into our car and drive away to the airport. The car took another 15 mins to show up as “the valet was not on duty”. Mind you the parking garage is gigantic, so it’s not really possible to find your car if you haven’t parked it yourself (we made the mistake of having the valet park it at check in the previous night) 
- asked for the manager’s email to lodge a strong formal complaint but was given some random email address from which mails bounced back 

I have never really written a review for a hotel as no place I’ve ever stayed at has managed to disappoint and frustrate me in such a spectacular manner. 

In short, AVOID this hotel at all costs - stay somewhere else and save your sanity.

reviews.title: Subject: A Highly Disappointing Experience at Alseef Heritage Hotel
reviews.text:
I am writing to express my profound disappointment regarding my recent stay at Alseef Heritage Hotel. Unfortunately, my experience fell far below my expectations.

Firstly, the room I had booked, with its promised balcony and creek view, turned out to be quite disappointing. The air conditioning system in the room was ineffective and failed to provide the necessary cooling, making my stay uncomfortable. Furthermore, the room felt cramped and stuffy, which further added to my dissatisfaction.

Moreover, I had to endure the constant sound of Indian drums emanating from the neighboring Indian restaurant throughout my stay. Despite raising this concern with your staff, I was informed that the hotel had no control over the noise. This response left me feeling disappointed and frustrated, as I believe the hotel should strive to ensure a peaceful environment for its guests.

To address the situation, I was offered the option to move to your sister hotel, Canopy. Regrettably, the room provided at Canopy did not match the quality and value of the originally booked room. It was disheartening to experience a downgrade in accommodations, and to my dismay, even the air conditioning in the new room took a considerable amount of time to cool down.

Overall, my stay at Alseef Heritage Hotel did not meet the standards I anticipated. As a paying guest, I expected a level of comfort and service that aligned with the price and the promises made during the booking process.

reviews.title: A Cultural Gem in Old Dubai: Hilton's Unique Experience
reviews.text:
My recent stay at the Hilton in Old Dubai left me with mixed feelings. Perhaps my expectations were set too high for a Hilton hotel, but there were certainly aspects that stood out and made it a memorable experience.

The location of the hotel is a definite highlight. Nestled in the heart of old Dubai, it offers a unique opportunity to immerse yourself in the rich culture of the city. Exploring this historic part of Dubai was a satisfying experience, even though my two-night stay happened to fall on a weekend when many shops were closed. However, the charm of the area is undeniable, and early morning strolls along its picturesque streets, devoid of crowds, were truly peaceful.

The hotel itself exuded an antique charm that I found quite appealing. Even the quirkiest details, such as the bathroom, added to the overall ambiance. However, I must mention a rather unpleasant issue – the carpet in my room had an off-putting smell, reminiscent of dried urine. Despite my efforts to air it out by placing it outside, the housekeeping staff repeatedly returned it to its original place. This unfortunate odor forced me to keep my windows open throughout my stay.

Another concerning discovery was the mysterious red substance smeared in the bathroom. While I didn't want to dwell on it too much to avoid dampening my holiday spirit, it was a detail worth mentioning. Whether it was blood or spices, it left me with questions.

One aspect of the hotel that took me by surprise was the layout. I only realized upon arrival that the hotel had a compound-like structure, requiring guests to take a buggy to reach their rooms. While this was an interesting experience, it might not be suitable for everyone, especially those with mobility concerns.

Despite these hiccups, the standout feature of the Hilton in Old Dubai was its staff. The service I received was superb, and the staff were always ready to accommodate my needs. Their professionalism and hospitality added to the overall positive impr

Best Regards, 
Mahmoud Elbendary

reviews.title: Stay away from room 222
reviews.positives:
Booked a half board creek view with balcony:

Hotel and room: an easy 9
Location is perfect, specially for a stop over. The ambiance of the hotel is just perfect, the surrounding Souq and the creek view from the balcony specially when a dhow passes by just enriches the whole experience. All that takes you back in time for a glimpse of what life used to be. The room furniture didn't disappoint either, the old phone, radio, ceiling, even the bathroom. You literally back to the 60s and 70s. 
Breakfast:  a 7.5
Got the basics covered, nothing specially here, attentive and friendly staff,  would've been  nice if they had any local dish to complement the whole experience.
reviews.negatives:
Reception: 2
couldn't careless about your wishes and here is why. when I booked, I emailed them for calm/quite room away from noise as it is a top priority for me and I also asked them for a 13p-14pm check-in which they didn't fulfill. That's absolutely their right and I perfectly understand that. Anyway We check-in at 3pm and we were really happy with the room except for a water hose problem which they fixed within 2 hours. BUT come 4pm and the restaurant next to Hotel got Live DRUMs and hardly any "music" playing. The drummer was banging the drums so hard as if his life depended on it "picture attached",  I thought this might be a one hour thing and will finish but Nooo, it lasted until 11pm at night during which and this is what REALLY pissed me off, I went to the reception after dinner around 9pm and I told him I got an issue with the room, he asked me what room am I in, once I told him its 222, he said, "yes the live music!!" I asked him for any solution, offered to downgrade my room since there is no room with balcony like the one I booked or bare it out one more hour and the music will stop, There was no apology or even empathy in the way they talk!! so I left and wrote a note through Booking to them but none responded. Next day at check out, there was the guy who originally booked me the room the day before whom I told him I needed a quiet room, he asked me how was my stay, I just shook my head off, didn't say a single word.  He kept quite and proceeded with the check out process, again no empathy whatsoever. Basically "All sizzle and no steak" attitude.

Room AC:  6
Room is not that cold, must rely on fan.

Dinner : 6
Ordered a wagyu burger, consisted of a slice of tomato, lettuce and bun with a very thick patty of over cooked wagyu beef, no pickle, so sauce, no ketchup, no mayo, nothing, not even on the side, basically the chef butchered the burger.

reviews.title: A hotel overflowing with an exotic atmosphere.
reviews.text: The hotel is located in a corner of the tourist area of Al Seef, featuring a two-story building with rooms lined up side by side. The area around the hotel has souks, restaurants, and cafes that are bustling until late at night, making it safe for families with children to stroll around. The night view along the river is beautiful. The rooms have a charming atmosphere and are somewhat dim. The family suite is designed with connecting rooms and has two shower sinks available; upon request at check-in, they provided a supplementary bed. Cleaning is only done if you put out the cleaning sign. The location is convenient, with boats departing nearby to the souk on the opposite bank. It takes about 20 minutes by taxi to reach the center, such as Dubai Mall, and approximately 30-40 minutes to the airport.

reviews.title: Could do better
reviews.text:
During my stay, I encountered three primary concerns that significantly impacted my comfort:

1.  Room Amenities and Cleanliness: Upon using one of the towels provided in my room, I discovered it was severely torn and frayed. This was disappointing as it reflects poorly on the hotel's housekeeping standards and attention to detail, which are crucial for a property celebrating heritage and quality. 

2.  Noise Insulation:  The noise insulation from the hallway and the streets was notably poor. I was consistently disturbed by noises from outside and the corridor, which made it very difficult to relax and get a restful night's sleep.

3. Bathroom Maintenance: The shower in my bathroom had poor drainage, causing water to pool around my ankles while showering. This was both unpleasant and a potential safety hazard.

I have tried to feedback to the hotel privately by email, but there was no reply,  which also signifies that the hotel is not doing their best to improve on their facility.

reviews.title: Al Seef Heritage Hotel
reviews.text: This is my second time visiting Dubai in a year, and I'm currently staying here for a week and a half at this point. I rented room at the Al Seef Heritage Hotel Curio by Hilton, and had a rather good experience. The first room that I checked into I had mild issues with, but spoke to the receptionist who then in return move me to a different room. I found the rooms to be rather dark but overall very clean, and the location isn't the old part of Dubai and seems rather historic. If you want to immerse yourself in an area where to buy first cut its root, with a little history and a lovely market below this is the place to stay. I must say what really made my stay was the hospitality of certain individuals that worked at the hotel. This is really what made my stay valuable as I too have worked in the hospitality field, and do know the ins and outs of the business. It wasn't just one individual that was accommodating and cordial, it was many individuals who actually turned what little bad situation I had into a rather pleasant stay. Those individuals were Dixon, Alan, Rameyet, an Indian receptionist ( Didn't catch her name) and a lovely greeter at the restaurant that I had breakfast at. I cannot remember for the life of me the name of the restaurant but that too was well worth the money that I paid to eat for my partner and I. Although I didn't feel well and didn't eat the second day, the food was exceptionally well and I do dabble in cooking, and have quite a bit of experience with that line. It was a buffet but pretty much anything that you can imagine ( all homemade for the most part) was available and you could eat as much as you wanted. I really enjoyed the fresh watermelon juice the honeys the maple syrups the wide variety of authentic food and food that I am used to from United States as well. I think his name was Alan he was a rather skinny young male who made my stay so pleasurable. He even brought fruits and a chocolate bar in my room to check on me, and he did that not only once but he also checked on me many times within the restaurant and asked me how my stay was, such a very charming, and accommodating young man, and definitely an asset to the hotel. I also must say that the drivers that drove us around in the little golf carts were very friendly, and wouldn't even let you carry a bag even though I demanded lol. So overall it was a pleasant experience and would stay there again because of the staff. I'm sure if I had told them that I was sick on the second day and didn't eat breakfast they probably would have refunded it that's how accommodating they were. Anyways if you would like clean rooms and a historic part of Dubai with good breakfast and friendly staff this is the place to stay.

reviews.title: Nice hotel in historic district
reviews.text: The rooms are spread across different buildings in the al Fahidi historic district.  Rooms have traditional design and each is designed slightly differently.  We had originally booked a creek view room but upon going to the room, there was no creek or any view except that of a canopy.  The room was some distance from the main lobby and upon phoning to clarify the room, I was told that there were no creek view rooms available and  the one that was, had plumbing problems.   Eventually, the hotel compensated us with offering free breakfast and then moving to a creek view room with a balcony.  However, I think such information should be told to the guest immediately at check in so that we can discuss alternative options.  Also, as the room was not booked at the last minute, I think the hotel can improve in making sure it allocates the correct type of room to each guest, as the cost of different rooms do differ.  It was a bit of a hassle to half unpack, then re-pack and move to another room.  I would also like to suggest that housekeeping be consistent in what they replenish to the room each day.  Upon check in, the room with twin beds (so for 2 people) had 3 wooden stirrers for drinks.  We used 2.  But they were never replenished.  Surely they do not expect us to then use only 1 stirrer for the next 2 days?  May I suggest that the hotel supplies proper spoons so that we can wash them and reduce waste.  Overall, the rooms were nice with comfortable beds, although the rooms were too dark for reading.  We visited in November and the weather wasn't very hot.  The rooms stayed cool after sunset and we didn't need AC at night.

reviews.title: Overall it's good, but I was disappointed with the front staff.
reviews.text:
The hotel itself was very nice.

However, I had an urgent matter with the front desk staff. When I approached them, I was told to "wait," and I waited for about 10 minutes, but no one from the front desk spoke to me. I had to initiate the conversation again to get assistance.

Even when checking in, it wasn't that crowded, but the process took about 10 to 15 minutes, so I think it's better to plan your schedule with some extra time.

The room was beautiful from any angle, and I was happy to find a well-stocked minibar. The presence of a ceiling fan helped with drying laundry and made it comfortable.

The downsides were that the room was generally dark (possibly because the concept was based on the old town), the shower room didn't have a door which resulted in a wet bathroom floor, and the shower drain was somewhat clogged.

On the positive side, as mentioned earlier, the hotel is lovely everywhere you take a picture, and the breakfast buffet was delicious. It was also nice to be able to easily buy souvenirs at the nearby souk. Depending on the shops in the souk, you can buy Dubai chocolate for about half the price compared to airport stores, so I recommend buying it there!

The nearest metro station is BurJuman, and from there, it's a 10-15 minute taxi or bus ride, so if you're using this hotel as a base for sightseeing, you might not use the metro much.

It’s recommended for those who mainly want to enjoy the souk, prefer using taxis for transportation, or have a fixed itinerary with booked tours!

reviews.title: Road trip from Qatar
reviews.text:
The overall experience was great. 

The souq was bustling until late hours and the room was right adjacent to it. 

The curator has done a splendid job. The rustic feel is right on the money. No qualms about the actual room. 

The breakfast spread was bit too even across 4- day stay. The fruits and breads literally didn't differ one bit on any day. 

The swimming pool is way too far and I've been to better ones. 

Waiting for the buggy to move around and vallet parking was bit off. We would lose 30 minutes straight on this process. 

I had texted about the tentative check in time. Still, we had to wait for an hour to get into the room. There was clearly shortage of staff. Yes, it was post Eid rush week. But that was not properly anticipated.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
I absolutely loved the location of the hotel, and I must give special mention to the gentleman who assisted us during check-in and check-out. Unfortunately, I didn’t catch his name, but he was a kind and lovely man of African descent who truly made our experience feel welcoming.

Upon arrival, we were taken to our room by buggy. While the ride made it seem like the hotel was a bit far from the lobby, we later realized it was just a short 2-3 minute walk. The location around the souk was beautiful and added to the charm of our stay.

However, there was a significant issue with noise during the night. From around midnight to 1:30 a.m., we experienced loud hammering, drilling, and laughter coming from underneath the hotel. Initially, we hoped it would stop, but it persisted and made it impossible to sleep. We tried calling the reception multiple times, but unfortunately, there was no response. Eventually, we reached out via the Hilton service chat, and—whether coincidental or not—the noise stopped about 15 minutes later.

While we enjoyed many aspects of our stay, this disturbance was disappointing and disrupted what would otherwise have been a perfect experience. I hope measures can be taken to ensure such incidents are avoided in the future. Thank you.

For those who love taking photos, whether inside the room or throughout the entire neighborhood,
there are many places full of traditional features.
Although it's a bit far from the subway station, about a 15-minute walk,
buses and taxis are very convenient.
There are many restaurants and supermarkets along the way, with affordable prices.
The entire neighborhood is developed as a tourist spot,
so there are many shops selling souvenirs downstairs in the building.
There are even antiques to treasure hunt for.
The restaurant prices are on the higher side, but the souvenir shops are diverse and cheap.
There are many tall buildings.
The building I stayed in this time is very close to the most beautiful Starbucks.
When passing by, many tourists specifically come here to take pictures.
It's a bit of a distance from the reception,
but walking slowly is fine, and there's a free shuttle within the area.
The faucet is a bit old, but the overall room is clean.
The air conditioning is sufficient, hot water is abundant, and the bed is comfortable to sleep on.
This is the only place during the whole trip that provides slippers and toiletry supplies.

Eventhough it was just a one night stay, soon as taxi dropped me off, a hotel staff came immediately to assist with my luggage and give a welcoming drink. Receptionist also upgraded my room which had a good view of the deira creek and the street below. 

Do expect that you'll need a cart to get to your rooms specially if your luggage is heavy. It's a 2-3 minute walk back to reception.

The room has a nice cultural touch and the hotel is perfectly located in the heart of the district and a short walk from Al Fahidi Historical Neighborhood as well as Abra station heading to the Gold and Spice Souk. 

It's far from the Marina though but an okay distance to the other tourist sites

The staff who assisted me were all lovely and very helpful.

We had a fantastic stay at Al Seef Heritage Hotel. The hotel perfectly captures the charm and heritage of old Dubai, while still offering all the comfort you need for a relaxing stay. The rooms are beautifully designed, clean, and full of character.

The staff were extremely friendly, welcoming, and helpful, always ready to assist with a smile. The location is excellent, right in the heart of Al Seef, with great restaurants, shops, and a lovely atmosphere just outside the door.

reviews.title: Cute hotel in the newly restored Al Seef District
reviews.positives: This cute hotel in the newly restored Al Seef neighborhood was a good base for our week's trip to Dubai, as many attractions were nearby and taxis always abundant. The property was charming (fitted up with historic-looking details), and the breakfast buffet was amazing! The beds were comfortable and the bathrooms great.
reviews.negatives: The lighting in the room was so poor that I had to use my cellphone light to read my guidebook when it got dark around 6:00 p.m. It was dark like being in a tent with a lantern. The lighting as a MAJOR issue for us: we also couldn't find small things in our suitcases any night, and we didn't have sufficient lighting to repack for the trip home during the evening (had to return to our room early to do this during daylight).  The poor lighting meant that hanging out in the room after sundown felt like a slumber party.  This is ridiculous, as I suspect lighting is an issue for MANY travelers (would likely be even worse for retirees/older guests [which we are not]). We asked the front desk to replace the faulty bedroom lightbulb; after this happened, the lighting was still just as dim. We also asked if they could provide us with an extra lamp to supplement lighting (yet, didn't get one). We got the impression that the poor lighting in the room was part of the intentional "mood" intended to indicate "Old Town Arabia."  This may've been okay if we were camping.  But, we were not.  Since the room was poorly lit, the fact that it didn't have a small seating area in our twin room made it worse.

reviews.title: The best 8th Wedding Anniversary Celebration
reviews.text:
A Memorable Anniversary Stay at Al Seef Heritage Hotel by Hilton – Simply Perfect!

We had the pleasure of celebrating our 8th wedding anniversary at Al Seef Heritage Hotel by Hilton, Dubai, and it was an unforgettable experience from start to finish!

Although we booked through Agoda.com, the hotel went above and beyond by upgrading our room to one with a small balcony facing the beautiful creek — such a thoughtful gesture that made our stay even more special.

From the moment we arrived, the staff were incredibly welcoming, friendly, and accommodating. We were greeted with a refreshing hibiscus tea juice and escorted to our room in a charming buggy, which added to the authentic feel of the heritage setting.

The room was spotlessly clean, and we absolutely loved the traditional interior design, reflecting the rich culture of old Dubai. Every detail was thoughtfully curated, and we felt transported to another time — but with modern comfort.

The food was delicious, and everything about our stay was perfect in our eyes. We felt so well taken care of and appreciated every bit of the hospitality shown to us.

We will definitely be back again and highly recommend this hotel to anyone looking for a unique and memorable experience in Dubai.

Thank you so much, Al Seef Heritage Hotel, for making our anniversary so special!

reviews.title: Wonderful historical location
reviews.positives: This is a beautiful historical setting, but the hotel has all the modern facilities you could want, and everything was in good working condition, including excellent air conditioning and ample hot water supply.. If you are looking for marble bathtubs and gold plated taps, it is not the hotel for you, but I loved the quirky historical decor, and the views over the rooftops of the souk.  You are surrounded by the market stalls every time you walk out from your room. and there is a good choice of restaurants and coffee shops nearby. The hotel provided electric buggies to take guests to and from the rooms with their luggage, and down to the docks for the ferry crossing of the creek. The restaurant has lovely indoor and outdoor seating, and the buffet breakfasts were excellent quality. Service by all staff was exceptionally good. Ideal location if you want to see more of traditional Dubai, but easy to reach other parts of the city by taxi, or ferry across the creek.
reviews.negatives: The souk is winding and complicated, and the hotel is spread over five different historical houses. I would have greatly appreciated receiving a map on check in with location of the restaurant, reception and the lifts to upper floors, with my room location marked on it.
